Parent A: Regular leaf
Parent B: Potato leaf

Step 2: Recognize the inheritance pattern
This crosses follow an Mendelian pattern (in the context of alleles), where one trait comes from each parent. 

Step 3: Apply the law of segregation
When considering only this trait, each parent has one copy of the gene determining leaf type. In a Punnett square, each parent can contribute either one copy or the other (regardless of the order or whether Parent A gives both 'regular' alleles first).
